SECURITY AFFAIRS MALWARE NEWSLETTER ROUND 101
Security Affairs newsletter Round 581 by Pierluigi Paganini – INTERNATIONAL EDITION
FBI disrupts massive AI-powered phishing service using a million URLs

executive_brief:
The threat landscape has fundamentally fractured. Traditional vulnerability management cycles are obsolete as AI-accelerated exploit development compresses the window between discovery and weaponization from months to hours. This week, Microsoft’s record-breaking Patch Tuesday addressing 206 flaws and three zero-days was immediately undermined by researcher Chaotic Eclipse, who dropped the RoguePlanet and GreatXML exploits that bypass freshly patched Windows Defender and BitLocker protections. Attackers no longer wait for patch deployment; they weaponize race conditions and recovery environment artifacts the moment vendors acknowledge a flaw.

Simultaneously, the extortion group ShinyHunters (UNC6240) executed a precision campaign against higher education, exploiting CVE-2026-35273 in Oracle PeopleSoft to breach over 100 institutions and exfiltrate billing and student finance data before Oracle could issue a mitigation. On the infrastructure front, Ivanti’s CVE-2026-10520 triggered immediate, unauthenticated root compromises of Sentry gateways hours after patch release, forcing CISA to issue Binding Operational Directive 26-04 mandating a three-day patch window for federal systems. These are not isolated incidents; they are symptoms of a coordinated shift toward supply chain poisoning, AI-agent manipulation, and zero-day chaining that renders perimeter defenses and static severity ratings meaningless.

Security teams must abandon reactive patching and adopt continuous control validation. The constraint is no longer finding vulnerabilities; it is operationalizing defense before autonomous worms and AI-driven campaigns chain low-severity misconfigurations into critical breaches. Organizations that do not implement breach-and-attack simulation, enforce strict network segmentation, and harden developer ecosystems against AI supply chain attacks will face systemic compromise within the next quarter.

threat_stories.headline: The Microsoft Zero-Day Cascade: When Patching Accelerates Exploitation
threat_stories.narrative:
Microsoft’s June 2026 Patch Tuesday was a defensive disaster. The vendor released updates for 206 vulnerabilities, including three actively exploited zero-days and critical flaws in HTTP.sys and the Windows kernel. The patch cycle collapsed within 48 hours. Security researcher Chaotic Eclipse (operating as Nightmare Eclipse) released RoguePlanet, a race condition exploit targeting Microsoft Defender that grants SYSTEM privileges on fully patched Windows 10 and 11 systems. The exploit manipulates Defender’s concurrent file-handling routines during ISO mounting operations, overwriting security binaries and spawning unrestricted shells.

Eclipse did not stop there. Within hours, the researcher published GreatXML, a BitLocker bypass that leverages artifacts left by Microsoft Defender’s offline scan feature. By placing a malicious unattend.xml file and Recovery directory on the Windows Recovery Environment (WinRE) partition, attackers trigger a SYSTEM shell during reboot, completely circumventing drive encryption. Independent analysis confirms the exploit functions on systems that have ever run a Defender offline scan, which includes the vast majority of enterprise endpoints. The vulnerability chain demonstrates a critical truth: patching one layer often exposes the next, and AI-assisted reverse engineering is accelerating exploit refinement beyond vendor response capabilities.

The operational impact is immediate. Defenders cannot rely on Patch Tuesday to close attack surfaces. Microsoft Defender’s core security routines are now predictable targets, and BitLocker’s encryption guarantees are compromised by recovery environment misconfigurations. Organizations must treat fully patched Windows environments as inherently vulnerable until Microsoft releases targeted mitigations for the race condition and WinRE XML processing flaws.

timeline.event: Microsoft releases record Patch Tuesday addressing 206 CVEs, including 3 zero-days and critical HTTP.sys/Kernel flaws.
timeline.significance: Establishes baseline patching expectation; immediately invalidated by subsequent disclosures.

timeline.event: Chaotic Eclipse publishes RoguePlanet exploit targeting Microsoft Defender race condition.
timeline.significance: Demonstrates that patched endpoints remain vulnerable to SYSTEM-level privilege escalation.

timeline.event: Eclipse releases GreatXML, a WinRE/Defender offline scan artifact exploit bypassing BitLocker.
timeline.significance: Neutralizes drive encryption on endpoints with standard security configurations.

timeline.event: Independent researchers validate GreatXML mechanics; Microsoft acknowledges investigation.
timeline.significance: Confirms functional exploit chain; forces enterprise reevaluation of offline scan policies.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: All fully patched Windows 10/11 environments are at risk of SYSTEM compromise and BitLocker bypass. Endpoint protection efficacy is severely degraded until vendor mitigations deploy.
threat_stories.action_required: Disable Microsoft Defender Offline Scan via Group Policy. Restrict ISO mounting permissions for standard users. Deploy alternative EDR solutions with behavioral anomaly detection. Monitor WinRE partitions for unauthorized XML modifications. Prepare for emergency Microsoft hotfix deployment.

threat_stories.headline: ShinyHunters’ PeopleSoft Extortion Spree: Weaponizing ERP Zero-Days
threat_stories.narrative:
The UNC6240 threat group, tracked as ShinyHunters, executed a highly targeted campaign exploiting CVE-2026-35273, a critical unauthenticated remote code execution flaw in Oracle PeopleSoft’s Environment Management component. Between May 27 and June 9, attackers compromised over 100 organizations across 300 vulnerable instances, primarily targeting U.S. and U.K. higher education institutions. The campaign bypassed traditional perimeter defenses by leveraging internet-exposed PSEMHUB endpoints, deploying disguised MeshCentral agents for command and control, and executing SSH credential spraying for lateral movement.

The operation was methodical and financially driven. Attackers exfiltrated billing records, student finance data, and administrative credentials, compressing stolen datasets with zstd before publishing extortion markers on their Data Leak Site. Oracle issued a mitigation on June 10, but CISA immediately added the flaw to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, recognizing that the exploit chain was already operationalized across threat actor networks. The campaign proves that enterprise ERP systems remain the highest-value targets for data extortion, and that zero-day exploitation in legacy application frameworks outpaces vendor response timelines.

Security teams must recognize that ERP platforms are no longer isolated back-office systems. They are internet-facing attack surfaces with hardcoded trust assumptions that AI models can chain into full server compromises. The ShinyHunters campaign will serve as a blueprint for other extortion groups, accelerating exploitation of similar RCE flaws in enterprise management suites.

timeline.event: ShinyHunters begins scanning and exploiting CVE-2026-35273 against university PeopleSoft instances.
timeline.significance: Marks start of zero-day campaign targeting higher education ERP infrastructure.

timeline.event: Campaign peaks; attackers exfiltrate data from 100+ organizations and deploy MeshCentral C2.
timeline.significance: Demonstrates fully operationalized exploit chain and lateral movement capabilities.

timeline.event: Oracle releases mitigation advisory for CVE-2026-35273.
timeline.significance: Vendor response arrives after active exploitation window closes.

timeline.event: CISA adds CVE-2026-35273 to KEV catalog; mandates federal remediation by June 15.
timeline.significance: Elevates flaw to critical national infrastructure risk; forces rapid enterprise patching.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Higher education, healthcare, and government sectors running Oracle PeopleSoft face active data theft and extortion. Internet-exposed PSEMHUB endpoints are directly compromised.
threat_stories.action_required: Immediately apply Oracle’s mitigation. Disable the Environment Management Hub service on multi-server setups. Block external access to /PSEMHUB/* and /PSIGW/HttpListeningConnector at the firewall. Audit WebLogic access logs for MeshCentral deployments and unauthorized SSH fanout scripts.

threat_stories.headline: Ivanti Sentry’s Day-Zero Compromise: When Patches Fail to Stop Exploitation
threat_stories.narrative:
Ivanti’s CVE-2026-10520 represents a catastrophic failure in secure software deployment. The flaw is an OS command injection vulnerability in Ivanti Sentry secure mobile gateways that allows unauthenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands with root privileges. Ivanti released patches for versions R10.5.2, R10.6.2, and R10.7.1 on June 10, but exploitation attempts hit honeypots within hours. By June 11, Shadowserver confirmed widespread wild exploitation, with attackers compromising gateways shortly after patch installation, indicating either a patch bypass or automated exploit distribution preceding vendor updates.

The vulnerability sits at the network perimeter, managing communications between corporate systems and mobile devices. Successful exploitation grants attackers direct access to the trusted enterprise boundary, enabling credential theft, lateral movement, and data exfiltration without triggering standard endpoint alerts. CISA responded by issuing Binding Operational Directive 26-04, mandating federal agencies to patch within three days, and added the flaw to the KEV catalog. The incident underscores a brutal reality: critical infrastructure appliances are being scanned and compromised in real-time, and vendor patch cycles no longer provide a safety buffer.

Organizations relying on Ivanti Sentry must treat their gateways as already compromised until verified otherwise. The flaw’s unauthenticated nature and root-level impact make it a prime target for ransomware affiliates and espionage operators. Network segmentation and immediate patch validation are no longer recommendations; they are survival requirements.

timeline.event: Ivanti releases patches for CVE-2026-10520 affecting Sentry R10.x gateways.
timeline.significance: Vendor attempts to close unauthenticated root RCE flaw.

timeline.event: Exploitation attempts hit honeypots; Shadowserver confirms wild exploitation.
timeline.significance: Proves exploit code is already operationalized and distributed.

timeline.event: CISA issues BOD 26-04 mandating 3-day patch window; adds CVE to KEV.
timeline.significance: Elevates to federal emergency; confirms active targeting of government infrastructure.

timeline.event: Security researchers report patch bypass attempts and secondary compromise vectors.
timeline.significance: Indicates attackers are adapting to patched environments, extending threat window.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: All internet-exposed Ivanti Sentry gateways face immediate root compromise. Enterprise network boundaries are breached, enabling lateral movement and data theft.
threat_stories.action_required: Deploy Ivanti patches immediately. Verify patch installation and restart gateway services. Restrict inbound network access to Sentry management interfaces via strict firewall rules. Monitor system logs for unauthorized root-level command execution and anomalous outbound C2 traffic. Assume compromise until forensic validation confirms integrity.

threat_stories.headline: The AI Supply Chain Avalanche: Poisoning Developer Ecosystems at Scale
threat_stories.narrative:
The software supply chain is under sustained, coordinated assault. This week, the Hades campaign compromised 19 Python packages on PyPI, embedding obfuscated scripts that drop a precompiled Bun runtime to execute JavaScript payloads and scrape OS memory for credentials. The malware evades AI-driven security scanners by injecting adversarial prompt instructions into code comments, forcing LLM triage systems to halt analysis or trigger safety refusals. Simultaneously, the Miasma worm trojanized the ai-sdk-ollama npm package, leveraging binding.gyp to deliver a self-replicating payload that propagates across development environments and steals CI/CD tokens.

The attack surface expanded to Linux desktop ecosystems when over 400 Arch User Repository (AUR) packages were hijacked to deploy an eBPF rootkit and infostealer. Attackers manipulate maintainer accounts and exploit the AUR’s decentralized trust model, granting persistent root access to users who install compromised packages. In response, GitHub announced npm v12 will disable automatic install script execution by default, a structural change forced by the Shai-Hulud worm and similar supply chain campaigns. The ecosystem is fracturing under the weight of automated dependency poisoning.

Security teams must recognize that package managers are now primary attack vectors. AI agents are being phished, code scanners are being blinded, and developer workstations are being compromised through trusted repositories. Organizations that do not implement strict dependency allowlisting, isolate build environments, and deploy hybrid scanning pipelines combining traditional static analysis with behavioral monitoring will face systemic credential theft and CI/CD pipeline compromise.

timeline.event: Hades campaign poisons 19 PyPI packages; employs LLM-Scanner Anti-Analysis techniques.
timeline.significance: Demonstrates active evasion of AI-driven security triage systems.

timeline.event: Miasma worm source code leaks; trojanized ai-sdk-ollama package propagates across npm.
timeline.significance: Confirms self-replicating supply chain malware targeting AI development stacks.

timeline.event: 400+ Arch Linux AUR packages hijacked to deploy eBPF rootkit and infostealer.
timeline.significance: Expands supply chain compromise to Linux desktop and developer tooling.

timeline.event: GitHub announces npm v12 will disable automatic install scripts by default.
timeline.significance: Vendor acknowledges systemic trust model failure; enforces architectural hardening.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Developer workstations, CI/CD pipelines, and AI agent deployments face immediate credential theft and persistent root compromise. Package ecosystems are actively poisoned.
threat_stories.action_required: Audit and remove compromised npm/PyPI/AUR dependencies. Rotate all exposed API keys, tokens, and credentials. Upgrade to npm v12 or enforce allowScripts=off. Isolate build environments from public networks. Deploy hybrid scanning pipelines that strip code comments before LLM analysis and combine signature-based detection with behavioral sandboxing.

threat_stories.headline: Geopolitical Campaigns & Infrastructure Exploitation: WinRAR, JDY, and Check Point
threat_stories.narrative:
State-aligned operators and ransomware affiliates are exploiting patched and deprecated infrastructure to maintain persistent access and launch extortion campaigns. Russian-aligned groups Earth Dahu and SHADOW-EARTH-066 continue exploiting CVE-2025-8088 in WinRAR, leveraging NTFS Alternate Data Streams to silently write malicious payloads to the Windows Startup folder. Despite a patch available for nearly a year, the flaw remains active due to unmanaged software environments in Ukrainian and Eastern European entities, demonstrating that patch availability is meaningless without centralized software inventory and automated deployment.

Simultaneously, China-linked operators behind the JDY botnet expanded to over 1,500 compromised IoT and SOHO devices, conducting selective reconnaissance against U.S. military networks and critical infrastructure. The botnet rapidly operationalized CVE-2026-35616 in Fortinet equipment within hours of disclosure, leveraging compromised routers for covert data transfer and pre-positioning for downstream attacks. On the ransomware front, affiliates of the Qilin group exploited CVE-2026-50751 in Check Point’s Remote Access VPN, bypassing IKEv1 authentication to establish unauthenticated VPN sessions and deploy encryption payloads within hours of initial access.

These campaigns are not isolated; they are interconnected infrastructure plays. Unpatched legacy protocols, unmanaged endpoint software, and exposed network appliances provide the footholds that enable large-scale espionage and extortion. Organizations that fail to segment IoT networks, enforce protocol modernization, and implement strict application control will continue to serve as staging grounds for geopolitical and financial campaigns.

timeline.event: Check Point confirms active exploitation of CVE-2026-50751 by Qilin ransomware affiliates.
timeline.significance: Deprecated IKEv1 protocol exploited for unauthenticated VPN access and rapid encryption.

timeline.event: JDY botnet expands to 1,500+ devices; begins scanning for CVE-2026-35616 in Fortinet equipment.
timeline.significance: China-linked reconnaissance targets U.S. military networks; rapid vulnerability operationalization.

timeline.event: Earth Dahu and SHADOW-EARTH-066 continue exploiting patched WinRAR flaw CVE-2025-8088 in Ukraine.
timeline.significance: Proves unmanaged software environments sustain long-term exploitation despite vendor patches.

timeline.event: CISA adds Cisco SD-WAN, Arista EOS, and Chrome V8 flaws to KEV catalog.
timeline.significance: Confirms multi-vendor infrastructure exploitation is systemic and actively weaponized.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Ukrainian/Eastern European entities face persistent credential theft and wiper deployment. U.S. military and critical infrastructure face reconnaissance and pre-positioning. Enterprises using deprecated VPN protocols face immediate ransomware deployment.
threat_stories.action_required: Update WinRAR to v7.13+; enforce application whitelisting and restrict archive extraction privileges. Disable IKEv1 on all Check Point devices; enforce IKEv2+. Segment IoT/SOHO networks; block outbound traffic from unmanaged devices. Apply vendor patches for Fortinet CVE-2026-35616 and Cisco SD-WAN CVE-2026-20245 immediately.

actor_profiles.name: ShinyHunters (UNC6240)
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Executed precision campaign exploiting CVE-2026-35273 in Oracle PeopleSoft, breaching 100+ higher education institutions. Deployed MeshCentral C2, executed SSH credential spraying, and published extortion markers on Data Leak Site.

actor_profiles.name: Chaotic Eclipse (Nightmare Eclipse)
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Released RoguePlanet (Defender race condition -> SYSTEM) and GreatXML (WinRE/BitLocker bypass) immediately following Microsoft Patch Tuesday. Demonstrated functional exploits against fully patched Windows 10/11.

actor_profiles.name: JDY Botnet Operators
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Expanded botnet to 1,500+ compromised IoT/SOHO devices. Rapidly scanned for and operationalized CVE-2026-35616 in Fortinet equipment. Targeting U.S. military networks and critical infrastructure for covert data transfer.

actor_profiles.name: Earth Dahu / SHADOW-EARTH-066
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Continued exploitation of patched WinRAR flaw CVE-2025-8088 using NTFS Alternate Data Streams to deploy credential stealers and wipers targeting Ukrainian entities. Campaign persists due to unmanaged endpoint software.

actor_profiles.name: Hades Campaign Operators
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Poisoned 19 PyPI packages with obfuscated scripts dropping Bun runtime for memory scraping. Employed LLM-Scanner Anti-Analysis techniques using adversarial prompt injection to evade AI security triage. Propagated via GitHub Actions and OIDC.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Unauthenticated remote code execution in the Environment Management Hub (PSEMHUB) component. Attackers with network access can fully compromise the server without user interaction.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply Oracle security patch released June 10, 2026. Disable PSEMHUB service. Block external access to /PSEMHUB/* and /PSIGW/HttpListeningConnector.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: OS command injection vulnerability allowing unauthenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands with root privileges on the gateway appliance.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to versions R10.5.2, R10.6.2, or R10.7.1 immediately. Restrict inbound network access via firewall rules.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Out-of-bounds memory access in the V8 JavaScript engine allowing remote arbitrary code execution within the browser sandbox via crafted HTML.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Google Chrome to version 149.0.7827.103 or later immediately. Enable automatic updates.

critical_vulnerabilities.product: Check Point Remote Access VPN / Mobile Access
critical_vulnerabilities.description: Logic flow weakness in certificate validation during IKEv1 key exchange allowing unauthenticated remote attackers to bypass password authentication and establish VPN sessions.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply emergency hotfixes. Disable deprecated IKEv1 protocol and enforce IKEv2 or higher for all remote access connections.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Command injection in MCP server testing endpoints allowing authenticated users to execute arbitrary OS commands, chainable to unauthenticated RCE.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to version 1.83.7 or later. Restrict API key permissions and enforce strict RBAC. Disable MCP testing endpoints.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Path traversal vulnerability in POST /api/v2/ endpoint allowing unauthenticated attackers to write arbitrary files to the server filesystem, leading to RCE.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update to latest patched version. Restrict network access via firewall/WAF. Deploy WAF rules to block path traversal sequences.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Use-after-free vulnerability in nf_tables packet-filtering subsystem enabling local privilege escalation to root and container breakout.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Linux kernel to version containing patch released upstream February 5, 2026. Reboot affected systems to load patched kernel module.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Path traversal flaw allowing attackers to bypass directory boundaries and write files to system directories via NTFS Alternate Data Streams.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update WinRAR to version 7.13 or later. Enforce strict application control policies and restrict archive extraction privileges.

hunting_leads.title: RoguePlanet & GreatXML Exploitation Indicators
hunting_leads.context: Hunt for evidence of Defender race condition exploitation and WinRE partition manipulation bypassing BitLocker.
hunting_leads.query: index=windows_security EventCode=4688 (CommandLine="*mountvol*" OR CommandLine="*diskpart*" OR CommandLine="*unattend.xml*") OR (FileName="*Defender*" AND ProcessCommandLine="*race*" OR ProcessCommandLine="*vhd*") | stats count by dest, process_name
indicators: SYSTEM shell spawning from recovery environment

hunting_leads.title: Oracle PeopleSoft PSEMHUB Exploitation
hunting_leads.context: Detect ShinyHunters campaign activity targeting CVE-2026-35273 and MeshCentral C2 deployment.
hunting_leads.query: index=web_proxy OR index=firewall src_ip=* dst_port=443 (url="/PSEMHUB/*" OR url="/PSIGW/HttpListeningConnector") | stats count by src_ip, dst_ip | where count > 5
indicators: SSH credential spraying from PeopleSoft hosts

hunting_leads.title: Ivanti Sentry CVE-2026-10520 Exploitation
hunting_leads.context: Identify unauthenticated root command execution attempts against Ivanti Sentry gateways.
hunting_leads.query: index=network_security OR index=firewall src_ip=* dst_port=443 (url="*Sentry*" OR url="*mobilegateway*") AND (action=allow OR status=200) | stats count by src_ip, dst_ip | where count > 3
indicators: Unauthenticated API hits to Sentry management interface
Anomalous outbound C2 traffic from gateway

hunting_leads.title: AI Supply Chain & Package Poisoning
hunting_leads.context: Detect Hades campaign, Miasma worm, and AUR hijacking activity targeting developer environments.
hunting_leads.query: index=endpoint (process_name="bun" OR process_name="npm" OR process_name="pip") AND (command_line="*install*" OR command_line="*download*") | search NOT (user="svc_build*" OR user="admin*") | stats count by host, process_name, command_line

hunting_leads.title: Check Point IKEv1 Authentication Bypass
hunting_leads.context: Hunt for Qilin affiliate exploitation of CVE-2026-50751 and unauthenticated VPN session establishment.
hunting_leads.query: index=vpn_logs protocol="IKEv1" (status="success" OR action="allow") AND (auth_method="certificate" OR auth_method="password_bypass") | stats count by src_ip, dst_ip, user | where count > 2
indicators: Successful IKEv1 connections without valid passwords

statistics.key_changes: AI-accelerated exploit development has collapsed the vulnerability-to-weaponization timeline to hours. Patch Tuesday no longer closes attack surfaces; it triggers immediate researcher and adversary exploitation cycles. Supply chain poisoning now targets AI security scanners directly, blinding automated triage systems.
emerging_trends: AI agent prompt injection achieving 41-68% success rates in indirect attacks
LLM-Scanner Anti-Analysis techniques using adversarial code comments to bypass AI triage
Zero-day chaining across ERP, VPN, and endpoint protection layers within 24-hour windows
Shift from reactive CVSS-based patching to exploitability-driven control validation
declining_threats: Traditional email phishing campaigns (displaced by AI-branded social engineering and supply chain poisoning)
Static vulnerability scanning (ineffective against logic flaws and AI-generated variant analysis)
Perimeter-only defense models (neutralized by unauthenticated RCE in ERP and gateway appliances)
